scriptText:
PART 1

[[STAGING]]
[[GLOBAL]]
Create a 15-second opening scene for a continuous take video featuring a live-action character, MARK, in a messy, living room. The scene should emphasize "raw reality" through gritty textures, messy wardrobe, and slow, organic handheld camera movement that transitions from a close-up of clutter to a medium shot of Mark enjoying a lazy, unpolished Sunday. 
[[/GLOBAL]]
[[LOOKBOOK]]
CHARACTER WARDROBE
MARK is dressed in "Aggressively Mundane" attire. He wears a faded, slightly stretched-out mustard yellow t-shirt with a small, unidentifiable oil stain near the hem. His pants are oversized, dark-gray fleece joggers that have pilled significantly at the knees. He wears one white sock and one grey sock—both sliding down his ankles. His hair is a "nest" of morning-frizz, and his glasses are slightly crooked. He looks like a man who hasn't looked in a mirror for three days, and he’s perfectly happy about it.

ESTABLISHING SCENE
The living room is a sanctuary of functional clutter. It’s a "lived-in" space that hasn't seen a vacuum in a week. Sunlight filters through a dusty window, revealing floating motes of dust in the air. The primary prop is a sagging navy-blue fabric sofa, its cushions permanently indented by Mark’s weight. The coffee table is the "altar of the mundane": a ceramic mug with a brown ring of dried coffee at the bottom, an open bag of generic potato chips with the clip missing, three different remote controls, and a tangled mess of white charging cables snaking across the wood. A half-dead spider plant hangs in the corner, its leaves slightly yellowed. The walls are an uninspiring off-white, adorned with a single, slightly tilted framed movie poster.

VISUAL BLOCKING
The shot begins as a Close-Up on the coffee table, focusing on a single potato chip being picked up by a shaky, unmanicured hand. As the hand retreats, the camera slowly pans and dollies back in a smooth, handheld motion to reveal MARK slumped in the center of the sofa. His body is angled 45 degrees toward a TV (off-camera). As he speaks, he leans forward slightly, his weight shifting the sofa cushions with a soft, mechanical creak of the springs. The camera continues to circle him in a slow 180-degree arc, moving from his right side to his left, capturing the mess of the room behind him as he settles back into a state of total, unpolished relaxation.
[[/LOOKBOOK]]
[[/STAGING]]

INT. LIVING ROOM - DAY

The camera lingers on a messy coffee table. A hand with bitten fingernails reaches into a crinkly chip bag. SFX: LOUD, RUSTLING PLASTIC.
The camera DOLLIES BACK AND TILTS UP, following the hand to MARK’S face. He’s mid-chew, looking utterly vacant. He stares at the TV off-screen. SFX: A CRUNCH SO LOUD IT FEELS INTIMATE.

MARK:
(Mumbling through chips)
Finally. No notifications. Just peace.

He uses his t-shirt to wipe salt off his thumb. The camera PANS SLOWLY to his left, catching the dust motes dancing in the sun. Mark exhales, a long, heavy sound of a man who has finally clocked out of reality.

MARK:
No filters. No bullshit. Perfect.

He reaches for the coffee mug, his movement slow and heavy. The camera CIRCLES BEHIND his head, showing the messy crown of his hair against the backdrop of the cluttered room. As he brings the mug to his lips, he pauses, hearing a faint, digital chirp from the hallway.

PART 2

[[STAGING]]
[[GLOBAL]]
Create a 15-second "mixed-media" comedy scene for a continuous take where a live-action character (MARK) is physically displaced by his 2D-animated, idealized self (DAVE). The transition should feel like a corporate "upgrade" invading a private space. Use fast, natural banter and physical comedy as Dave "nudges" Mark out of his own seating area. Maintain the gritty, handheld aesthetic of the room to emphasize the sharp contrast with the vibrant, animated Dave.
[[/GLOBAL]]
[[CONTINUITY]]
CHARACTER WARDROBE
MARK: Same as the previous @VIDEO. 
DAVE: DAVE is the 2D-animated, "Idealized" version of Mark. He wears a sleek, form-fitting emerald-green turtleneck with zero wrinkles. His hair is perfectly styled with a gravity-defying blonde streak. His skin is flawless (no pores, no stubble), and his blue eyes have a constant, digital sparkle. He looks like he was rendered in 4K and pasted into the 1080p, dusty reality of the room.

CONTINUITY PROTOCOL
Continue from @VIDEO to maintain absolute continuity. MARK is reclining on the sofa, his head resting against the back cushion. The pacing should flow seamlessly from previous scene @VIDEO, keeping the editing rhythm tight and the camera movement subtle. Ensure the spatial relationship between MARK and environment is preserved from the end of the previous shot.

VISUAL BLOCKING
The shot continues the slow ORBITAL PAN from @VIDEO. DAVE enters from SCREEN LEFT (the hallway door seen in the video). He walks behind the sofa with a smooth, "floaty" animation cycle. As he reaches the center, he places a hand on the sofa back and NUDGES Mark with his hip. MARK is forced to slide toward the SCREEN RIGHT (toward the window). By the end of this part, DAVE is seated comfortably in Mark’s original spot, while MARK is squeezed into the corner of the sofa.
[[/CONTINUITY]]
[[/STAGING]]

INT. LIVING ROOM - CONTINUOUS

The camera continues its slow, rhythmic orbit around the sofa. MARK is just settling into his sigh of relief. He reaches for the coffee mug, his movement slow and heavy. As he brings the mug to his lips, he pauses, hearing a faint, digital chirp from the hallway.

Suddenly, DAVE strides into the frame from the hallway. He looks sharp, vibrant, and offensively clean. He winces as he looks at Mark’s wrinkled shirt.

DAVE:
Sip looks messy. Try this.

MARK:
(Jerking upright, startled)
Wait, what—? Dave?

DAVE:
(Pushing Mark’s shoulder to the side)
I’m you, minus the pores.

Dave sits down on the cushion. SFX: A CLEAN, DIGITAL "POP" SOUND. Unlike Mark, Dave doesn't sink into the sofa; he stays "on top" of it.

MARK:
(Squeezed against the armrest)
I like my pores. Get out.

DAVE:
(Straightening his own perfect hair)
The audience doesn’t. Move over.

MARK:
This is my couch, man!

DAVE:
(Adjusting Mark’s crooked glasses with a finger)
It’s "unverified" content, Mark. Be grateful.

The camera PULLS BACK slightly as they bicker, framing both versions in a MEDIUM SHOT. MARK looks exhausted, while DAVE is practically glowing. 

MARK opens his mouth to argue further, but a wet, heavy THUD from the coffee table interrupts him.

PART 3

[[STAGING]]
[[GLOBAL]]
Create a 15-second "mixed-media" comedy scene for a continuous take where a heroic animated frog (LUCAS) displaces both a live-action human (MARK) and his animated counterpart (DAVE). The entrance should feel heavy and authoritative, using "Brand Safety" logic to justify the displacement. Use a combination of digital screen-shakes and wet foley sounds to give the frog a "physical" presence in the real-world room, ending with the human character literally pushed out of his own life.
[[/GLOBAL]]
[[CONTINUITY]]
CHARACTER WARDROBE
MARK: Same as the previous @VIDEO.
DAVE: Same as the previous @VIDEO.
LUCAS: He is a 2D-animated, humanoid frog standing about 3 feet tall. He wears a tattered, dark brown traveler’s cape with a rustic bronze clasp and a leather satchel across his chest. His skin is a deep, mossy green with pale yellow underbellies. His eyes are large, golden, and fixed in a bureaucratic stare. He looks like a high-quality fantasy character but acts like a corporate auditor. 

CONTINUITY PROTOCOL
Continue from @VIDEO to maintain absolute continuity. MARK is seated on the right side of the blue sofa, looking startled and annoyed at DAVE, who is leaning back on the left side of the sofa, looking smug. The camera is in a Medium Shot framing both. The transition begins exactly as the heavy, wet THUD vibrates the coffee table in the foreground. 

VISUAL BLOCKING
The shot remains part of the CONTINUOUS ONER. the camera TILTS DOWN to the coffee table to reveal LUCAS standing among the scattered mail and chip crumbs. He stares directly at the lens before turning to the others. The camera then TILTS UP and PULLS BACK into a WIDE SHOT. LUCAS hops with a heavy, wet sound from the table directly onto the sofa between MARK and DAVE. His weight (represented by a digital screen-shake) forces MARK to lose his balance and slide completely off the sofa onto the floor. DAVE is shoved toward the far-left armrest. By the end of this part, Lucas is the central figure on the couch.
[[/CONTINUITY]]
[[/STAGING]]

INT. LIVING ROOM - CONTINUOUS

MARK opens his mouth to argue further, but a wet, heavy THUD from the coffee table interrupts him. 
The camera captures the coffee table shaking from a sudden impact. LUCAS (Animated Frog) is suddenly there, standing on a pile of Mark's mail. SFX: A WET, SQUELCHING THUD.

DAVE:
(Recoiling)
Hey! I’m in the middle—

LUCAS:
Human likeness flagged. Too sensitive.

LUCAS hops from the table to the sofa. SFX: THE COUCH SPRINGS GROAN. The impact sends MARK sliding off the cushion and onto the hardwood floor. VFX: THE CAMERA SHAKES ON LANDING.

MARK:
(From the floor)
A frog? Seriously?

LUCAS:
(Looking down at Mark)
Low risk. High engagement.

DAVE:
(Gesturing to his face)
But I’m optimized! Look!

LUCAS:
(Turning to Dave)
Hair is a liability. Move.

LUCAS physically shoves DAVE with a webbed hand. DAVE slides to the very edge of the frame. The camera ZOOMS IN slightly on Lucas, who now owns the center of the sofa, looking majestic and immovable.

PART 4

[[STAGING]]
[[GLOBAL]]
Create the final 15-second climax of a "mixed-media" continuous take where the live-action human (MARK) accepts his total displacement by his animated filters. The scene should conclude with a feeling of "Digital House Arrest," using sharp, short dialogue and a final "corporate" sound effect to signify that the algorithm has won. The camera movement should slowly isolate Mark's face to emphasize his loss of control over his own environment.
[[/GLOBAL]]
[[CONTINUITY]]
CHARACTER WARDROBE
MARK: Same as previous @VIDEO. Mustard yellow t-shirt, gray joggers, messy hair, and crooked glasses. He is now physically lower than everyone else, sitting on the hardwood floor.
DAVE: Same as previous @VIDEO. 2D-animated, green turtleneck, blonde streak. He is squeezed into the far-left corner of the sofa, looking diminished.
LUCAS: Same as previous @VIDEO. 2D-animated frog in a traveler's cape. He sits dead-center on the sofa, chest puffed out, owning the frame.

CONTINUITY PROTOCOL
Continue from @f5b19916-1ccd-4728-81c2-6fce82334084 VIDEO to maintain absolute continuity. MARK is sitting on the floor to the right of the sofa, looking up in disbelief. LUCAS has just claimed the center seat, sitting with an air of absolute authority. DAVE is huddled on the left armrest. The bag of chips and scattered mail remain on the coffee table in the foreground.

VISUAL BLOCKING
The camera begins to SLOWLY CREEP FORWARD, tightening the frame from a Wide Shot into a Medium-Wide Shot that focuses on the three-way dynamic. LUCAS remains still, but his eyes track Mark’s movements. DAVE leans away from Lucas, trying to stay "in the frame" but failing. MARK shifts his weight on the floor, looking around his own room as if he’s a guest. At the 10-second mark, the camera DOLLIES IN to a Close-Up of Mark’s face to capture his final realization of defeat.
[[/CONTINUITY]]
[[/STAGING]]

INT. LIVING ROOM - CONTINUOUS

LUCAS (CONT'D):
(Turning to Dave)
Move.

LUCAS sits majestically. He spots a loose potato chip on the table. SFX: A WHIP-LIKE SNAP. His tongue shoots out and pulls the chip into his mouth.

MARK:
(From the floor)
Those were my organic chips.

LUCAS:
(Chewing slowly)
Optimizing the content, Mark.

DAVE:
(Whispering to Mark)
He has better engagement metrics.

Mark looks at Dave, then back at the frog. He looks at his own hands, then at the mess on the floor.

MARK:
I'm going for a walk.

Mark starts to stand up. SFX: A LOUD, STATIC BURST. Lucas raises a webbed hand, and the camera GLITCHES slightly.

LUCAS:
Don’t. Real world’s not verified.

Mark freezes mid-stand. He looks at the camera—his expression is one of pure, exhausted resignation. He slowly sits back down on the floor.

DAVE:
(Looking at the ceiling)
Should we... leave?

LUCAS:
(Staring at the lens)
Redacting.

Lucas lets out a deep, bass-boosted RIBBIT that sounds like a corporate jingle. The screen CUTS TO BLACK instantly.

cues.selectedText: A hand with bitten fingernails reaches into a crinkly chip bag.
The camera DOLLIES BACK AND TILTS UP
SFX: A CRUNCH SO LOUD IT FEELS INTIMATE.
uses his t-shirt to wipe salt off his thumb.
The camera PANS SLOWLY to his left
catching the dust motes dancing in the sun.
Dave sits down on the cushion.

cues.speaker: MARK
cues.selectedText: I like my pores. Get out.
LUCAS hops from the table to the sofa.
VFX: THE CAMERA SHAKES ON LANDING.
His tongue shoots out and pulls the chip into his mouth.
